lines: The wizard's spell missed, but the knight's sword found its mark.
The goblin dropped its weapon and begged for mercy, though no one believed it.
Rain hammered the old watchtower, and the torches struggled to stay lit.
The dragon circled twice before landing, and the ground shook when it did.
She could have run, but she chose to stand and fight instead.
The bridge swayed under their boots, yet somehow it held their weight.
He'd faced worse than this ogre, or so he kept telling himself.
The chest looked ordinary enough, but something about it made her pause.
The map was old and torn, and half the names had faded away.
The knight raised her shield just as the arrow whistled past.
Nobody spoke as the door creaked open, revealing only darkness beyond.
The potion tasted awful, but it worked exactly as promised.
Two roads split ahead, and neither one looked particularly safe.
The skeleton crumbled to dust, and the room fell silent again.
